channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ The sights of Modern Day Marine 2026 | Breaking Defense
item.description: Check out a selection of photos from the Modern Day Marine show floor.

item.title: 🌏 The Deeper Pattern Behind China’s Military Purges | Foreign Policy
item.description: Xi’s new commanders are the men his last generals blocked.

item.title: 🌍 Why Trump Might Come to Regret the Iran War | Foreign Policy
item.description: He hoped it would be transformational; instead, it’s just one more round in the conflict.

item.title: 🔐 Former head of ‘Pentagon’s think tank’ joins Anthropic | Defense One
item.description: The strategy expert calls adaptation to AI a "civilizational" challenge.

item.title: 🌍 At the 60-Day Mark, the Iran War is Triply Illegal | Just Security
item.description: Under the War Powers Resolution, Trump must now terminate the hostilities against Iran he began two months ago. He seems set against doing so. The post At the 60-Day Mark, the Iran War is Triply Illegal appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌍 The Price of Peace With Iran | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Washington must be willing to make uncomfortable concessions.

item.title: 📍 Lt. Gen. Doug Schiess tapped as 3rd Space Force chief | Breaking Defense
item.description: Lt. Gen. Doug Schiess will inherit a much larger Space Force, in both budget and end strength, than his predecessor Gen. Chance Saltzman.

item.title: 📍 How to Acknowledge Forced Marriage as a Standalone Crime in the Draft Crimes Against Humanity Treaty | Just Security
item.description: Adding forced marriage to the draft Crimes Against Humanity Convention would codify a distinct international crime, reflect its severe harms, and improve legal clarity. The post How to Acknowledge Forced Marriage as a Standalone Crime in the Draft Crimes Against Humanity Treaty…

item.title: 🌍 US Navy turns to AI firm Domino for options to counter Iranian mines | Defense News
item.description: The core of Domino’s pitch - and the Navy’s wager - is speed.

item.title: 📍 Legal Foundations of Honorable Military Service | Just Security
item.description: As former judge advocates, it was our responsibility while on active duty to advise on the legal demands of and limits on senior officers’ authority and responsibility. The post Legal Foundations of Honorable Military Service appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 Europe needs Ukraine as it looks to counter growing Russian threat | Atlantic Council
item.description: More than a year since Trump returned to the White House, it is clear that Europe needs to develop its own strategic vision for the continent’s defense. It is equally apparent that Ukraine must be central to this vision, writes Zahar Hryniv. The post Europe needs Ukraine as it…

item.title: 📍 The Real Meaning of the UAE’s OPEC Exit | Foreign Policy
item.description: The geopolitical realignment goes much deeper than just oil markets.

item.title: 📍 Colombia’s Anti-Fossil Fuel Conference | Foreign Policy
item.description: The country is trying to push for a green transition despite gridlock at U.N. climate talks.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: May 1, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei yesterday vowed to protect the Islamic Republic’s nuclear and…

item.title: 🕵️ Trump’s Plan B for Tariffs Rests on Shaky Foundations | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Section 301 case outlined by U.S. trade officials is neither coherent nor defensible.

item.title: 📍 Malaysia’s Tech Talent Shortage | The Diplomat
item.description: Industry leaders describe a war for talent.

item.title: 🏴 Modern Combat Requires Warrior Medics Modeled After Machaon | War on the Rocks
item.description: Modern peer combat has blown apart the myth of protected combat medical units. On the battlefields of Ukraine, scores of medical personnel, shielded in theory by both international law and historic norms, now lie dead. To survive in this environment while rescuing others, medics…

item.title: 🌍 Why Lebanon Is Nonnegotiable for Iran | War on the Rocks
item.description: As American and Iranian diplomats gathered in early April in Islamabad for Pakistan‑mediated ceasefire talks to end the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and its regional allies, including Hizballah, a sticking point emerged: whether the ceasefire was to include Lebanon. The United…

item.title: 🌏 The Philippines Joins Washington’s Pax Silica | The Diplomat
item.description: It's clear what the United States is getting out of its new critical minerals initiative, but less obvious how Manila stands to gain from it.

item.title: 🌏 Expanding frontiers: what happens if Beijing accelerates its Indo-Pacific push | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Rather than gradually expanding its defence and security engagement across the Indo-Pacific, Beijing may choose to accelerate its trajectory, pushing boundaries to advance its interests and take advantage of a distracted United States. The result ...

item.title: 🌏 China and America Are Courting Nuclear Catastrophe | Foreign Affairs
item.description: The consequences of Beijing’s weapons buildup.

item.title: 📍 Libya’s False Peace | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Libya needs political unity, not Washington’s dealmaking.

item.title: 📍 When Myanmar Exported Federalism | The Diplomat
item.description: After World War II, Burma helped design a federal constitution for Ethiopia and Eritrea, while fighting a civil war with ethnic minorities demanding the same at home.

item.title: 📍 3D-Printed Weapons: An Emerging Problem in Southeast Asia? | The Diplomat
item.description: While traditional firearms are currently a more pressing problem, seizures of 3DPFs are on the rise, with worrying implications for the region.

item.title: 📍 Cambodian Opposition Leader Loses Appeal Against Treason Conviction | The Diplomat
item.description: The Phnom Penh Appeals Court upheld Kem Sokha's 27-year house arrest sentence, while imposing a further five-year travel ban.

item.title: 🏴 Some US troops cite benefits of Germany presence as Trump threatens pullback | Defense News
item.description: Germany is the U.S. military’s largest footprint in Europe, with some 35,000 active-duty military personnel, and serves as a key training hub.

item.title: 🌏 Disinformation is Beijing’s weapon. Japan needs more than fact-checking to counter it | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Pro-China entities have attempted to use disinformation to undermine democracy and social cohesion in Japan. This has included targeting Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and her government through state media, diplomatic channels and coordinated online campaigns ...

item.title: 📍 Myanmar’s Ousted Leader Aung San Suu Kyi Transferred to House Arrest: State Media | The Diplomat
item.description: The announcement is part of a concerted PR push currently being undertaken by the new "civilian" government in Naypyidaw.

item.title: 🏛️ US combatant chiefs want more amphibious ready groups, Marine commandant says | Defense News
item.description: The four-star officers who lead U.S. military commands have all requested the support of amphibious ready groups and Marine Expeditionary Units.

item.title: 🌍 Ceasefire ‘stops’ War Powers clock on Iran, Hegseth claims | Defense News
item.description: The 60-day threshold is delineated in the War Powers Resolution of 1973. The most relevant part of that law makes no mention of a ceasefire.
